title: Oracle discriminator uses a random wrapper Name as collection key (AxFormExtensionControl) — FIXED
instruction: Add a form-extension control (add-control on objectType=form-extension) bound to CustGroup.ConHasNotes as a child of the Grid control. normalize.ts's discriminator() used to key the AxFormExtensionControl collection entry by its own randomly-generated wrapper <Name>, not by the nested stable name (FormControl/Name) -- so two independently-generated artifacts of the same logical control structurally false-mismatched even though they were logically identical. FIXED (2026-07-01): discriminator() now detects an auto-generated wrapper Name (looksAutoGenerated: <tag without Ax><lowercase/digit suffix>) and falls back to a nested stable Name/DataField (nestedName) instead. Verified live: this golden is a SECOND, independently-generated sample of the exact control captured in L2-form-extension-basic's own golden (different random wrapper id, delete+recreate+add-control replayed fresh) -- with the fix plus a "**/AxFormExtensionControl/Name" ignore glob for the still-differing wrapper-id leaf value, it scores golden_match=1 against that OTHER case's committed golden (cross-case acceptance-oracle proof).
